clean_story: Persistent connections.  Real-time data flow. "It" refers to WebSockets—a protocol that keeps a single, open line between your device and a server, like a phone call that never hangs up. Normally, websites ask for updates by shouting questions repeatedly—like knocking on a door every few seconds. WebSockets leave the door propped open. Messages fly both ways instantly:A stock ticker refreshes without reloading. A chat app shows replies the moment they’re typed. Even multiplayer games sync moves faster than blinking. Before WebSockets, live features relied on hacks—like sending tiny, hidden requests every second, draining batteries and bandwidth. Now, a single connection handles thousands of messages with less strain than a text thread. Three related subjects are data streams, low latency, and HTTP alternatives.
